ai_projection.projectName: Angel Delight — Inflatable Jelly Sampling Pop-up
ai_projection.location: Outdoor public area near family footfall (TBC — high-street pedestrian zone, shopping district, or family attraction)
ai_projection.dates: May Half Term — 1-day option and 3-day option (Thursday, Friday, Saturday)
ai_projection.summary: Branded inflatable jelly + ball pit pop-up activation sampling Angel Delight to families with tweens, with a built-in picture moment for organic social.

categories.oneLiner: Custom-printed inflatable jelly structure with attached ball pit (clear bubble dome), approx 4m tall, Angel Delight branded, engineered for public interaction — entry tunnel, integrated sampling station inside, ball-pit photo moment, weighted base for outdoor wind loading.

Free product sampling of Angel Delight in single-serve cups — refrigerated/temperature-controlled prep area, ~2,000 samples/day, food-hygiene certificated handlers, allergen labelling, branded sampling cups + spoons, refuse bins (recyclable streams).

4–6 brand ambassadors per day (one queue host, one sampler, two ball-pit attendants, one photographer/social capture), Angel Delight branded uniforms, briefed on key messaging and food-hygiene compliance.

Outdoor pitch hire in a high-footfall family location (shopping precinct, central square, or near a family attraction) — power supply, public-liability cover from the landlord, and licence to sample food.

RAMS for inflatable + ball pit (anchoring, child supervision ratios, ball-pit cleaning schedule), food-hygiene RA for product sampling, public-liability insurance, first-aider on site, daily kit inspection log.
Dedicated content creator on site to capture the ball-pit picture moment, family vox-pops, daily edit deliverables for paid social + earned media kit.
Angel Delight branded fascia print, queue-line vinyl, sampling station graphics, ingredient/allergen-info A4 boards, removable day-rate banners.
Van delivery + collection of inflatable, balls, sampling kit between depot and pitch; storage between 1-day and 3-day options; daily de-rig / re-rig if pitch not 24h secured.

topQuestions: Is the 1-day vs 3-day budget the same £40k each, or £40k total across both quotes?
Confirm location type (shopping precinct, public square, attraction forecourt) — affects pitch fees, wind ratings, and sampling licence path.
Is overnight de-rig required between days, or can the inflatable stay anchored and tarped on pitch?

ai_projection.projectName: Creditspring — Home a Loan Pop-up Shop
ai_projection.location: Blank-shell retail unit in Soho (client sourcing separately)
ai_projection.dates: Dressing 18 Nov · Launch 19 Nov · De-rig 20 Nov
ai_projection.summary: Festive 'Home a Loan' lending-shop pop-up dressed as a visually playful Christmas-takeover that drives press around the cost of Christmas and Creditspring's borrow-better narrative.

categories.oneLiner: Full festive shop dressing of a blank Soho unit — Home Alone-inspired styling (35th-anniversary cue), Christmas tree(s), decorated shelving, themed product displays representing 'rentable' Christmas must-haves; flexible install in ~1 day on 18 Nov.
Retail-style shelving, display plinths, counter / 'rental desk', festive seating area for storytelling vignettes; rented for the install + launch + de-rig window.
Warm festive lighting wash, fairy / festoon lights through the shop, accent lighting on photo moments and product displays, optional neon sign 'Home a Loan'.
Window vinyl 'Home a Loan' shopfront, branded price tags / rental-form collateral, branded merchandise, posters telling the 'real cost of Christmas' research story.
Stills + reel shoot covering shop dressing, launch day footfall, vox-pops with families, hero PR shots for tier-1 media drop. Same-day social edits.
2–3 brand hosts dressed in-keeping with the Home Alone festive cue, briefed on Creditspring messaging + rental mechanic; project manager across dressing, launch, de-rig.
RAMS for the install/de-rig, fire-loading check on dressing materials (festive props can be combustible), licence/insurance check with landlord, public-liability cover.
Delivery + collection of dressing, props, furniture; one rigger team for 18 Nov install and one for 20 Nov de-rig; minimum two transport movements.

topQuestions: Is the Soho unit confirmed and on what dates is access permitted (18–20 Nov only or earlier prep window)?
Are the 'rentable' items actually being lent out to the public, or are they styled props only? (Materially changes insurance + furniture spec.)
Is press / media drop happening on launch day or staggered — drives the content / photography spec.

ai_projection.location: British institution — Shakespeare's Globe or Science Museum (TBC, production to support sourcing)
ai_projection.summary: Half-day launch event at a British cultural institution showcasing 2–3 skills (STEM, literature, music) through TikTok creators, broadcast via TikTok LIVE, with teen attendees + media driving widespread launch coverage.

categories.oneLiner: Source, contract and manage a British institution in London (e.g. Shakespeare's Globe, Science Museum) with confirmed capability for TikTok LIVE broadcast; capacity 80–100; full morning hold 09:00–14:00 plus build-up/de-rig windows.
Three themed skill-station builds (STEM / literature / music) — modular so they can flex into Globe vs Science Museum footprints; branded backdrops to camera; TikTok green-screen photo moment.
Full broadcast-grade AV: multi-cam capture, vision mix, sound (lapels for talent, PA for room), TikTok LIVE-ready stream encode + uplink, monitors for in-room audience.
Broadcast lighting plot — soft key on talent at each skill station, backdrop wash, audience ambient lighting balanced for cameras.
Branded merch giveaway pack (teen-targeted), wayfinding signage, skill-station headers, press-wall step-and-repeat, badges for media + government stakeholders.
Production team (event manager + 2x producers), 4–6 hosts/runners for skill stations and registration, security/door for venue, project manager pre-event for talent + venue + media liaison.
RAMS for institution venue (insurance, evacuation, capacity limits), public-liability check, safeguarding plan for teen attendees (15–19), permits for broadcast and any external filming.
Stills photographer for press, dedicated content team for short-form social cut-downs through the morning; assets delivered same-day for media titles + TikTok channels.
Light breakfast on arrival + grab-and-go lunch for ~90 across talent + teens + media + stakeholders; dietary requirements collected at RSVP.
Get-in / install at venue (likely 22 July), de-rig same day after 14:00 close; storage of skill-station props between rehearsal + live.

topQuestions: What is the budget envelope? It is not stated and materially changes venue (Globe is significantly more than Science Museum room hire) and build spec.
Confirm TikTok LIVE technical spec — encode bitrate, uplink type (venue network vs hardline / 5G failover), and whether TikTok's own broadcast crew is in scope or production-provided.
Is talent + skill-session content delivered to production ahead of build, or are we building to generic skill-station templates?

ai_projection.summary: 10m × 12m sheltered cookalong activation at Latitude — back-to-back family cooking workshops 3pm–9pm with HelloFresh chef Mimi Morley + hired chef, plus daytime craft space, designed modular for reuse at smaller events.

categories.oneLiner: 10m × 12m weatherproof / sheltered festival build — fabricated structure able to operate outdoors in bad weather; modular components that demount, transport and reassemble for smaller events; evergreen HelloFresh branding (no Latitude-specific marks); removable banner panels for messaging swap.
Communal dining tables, family-style cookalong workstations, branded shelving for ingredient pre-portioning, decorative softs that read on camera, child-friendly craft area for daytime sessions.

Cooking equipment for ~6 family workstations × 4 days — induction hobs, mise-en-place stations, pre-portioned HelloFresh meal kits per 20-minute workshop, dishwash and waste flow, food-hygiene ratings for festival environment; supports back-to-back workshops 3pm–9pm.

Hired chef alongside Mimi Morley to run / support workshops; full pack-down + prep team between sessions; brand ambassadors for queue management + craft area; dedicated project manager (client has no in-house events manager). Day rates incl. weekend variations.
Demonstration cam + overhead cooking-station cam for in-space monitors so larger families can follow Mimi; sound system for workshop audio; on-site content capture rig for daily social edits.
Practical lighting on cooking stations (food-safe), festoon/ambient lighting across dining/craft area, dusk-to-close lighting that complements the structure aesthetic in low light.
Communal dining benches, family-size tables, workstation counters, craft-area seating, storage units for kits / consumables / cleaning materials.
Evergreen HelloFresh signage (not Latitude-specific); workshop schedule boards; recipe-card prints per workshop; child-friendly craft templates; removable messaging banners.
Festival H&S pack (fire, electrical, gas if applicable, public-liability) plus food-safety risk assessment for cookalong; child supervision policy; weather-contingency operating plan.
Truck movements from build yard to Latitude site; on-site storage between sessions; pre/post-event storage for the modular set; replenishment trips for ingredients during the festival.
Stills + reels for earned-media amplification (secondary objective); hero shots of cookalong, family vignettes; daily edits for paid social.

topQuestions: Is the £200k all-in (build + production + staffing + equipment + logistics) or does it exclude HelloFresh-supplied ingredients / Mimi's day rate?
Is the structure expected to live past Latitude as a touring asset (cost of reusability) or is it disposed after the festival?
Workshop pricing (£5–£10 per family to Felix Project) — does production handle ticketing infrastructure or is that client-side?

ai_projection.location: Battersea Power Station (1st choice) — Coal Drops Yard or Truman Brewery as alternatives
ai_projection.summary: Premium 12m-diameter circular sensory installation launching TABLÉ in the UK — four ritual zones (sight, aroma, sound, experience) plus a retail pre-order area, designed modular for reuse at sampling moments, partnerships and trade events.

categories.oneLiner: Circular installation, c.12m diameter, centred on the TABLÉ machine; four self-guided ritual zones (sight, aroma, sound, experience); premium finish, solid colour, clean architectural lines; modular so individual zones can flex for sampling activations, partnership moments and trade events.

Ritual-zone dressing — visual coffee display + light staging for Ritual 1; scent diffuser cabinet for Ritual 2 controlled aroma waves; soft acoustic treatment + speaker placement for Ritual 3 soundscape; ambient finishes throughout.

Multi-channel ambient sound system playing a sampled coffee soundscape (grind, pour, tamp, crema); programmable scent diffusion in controlled waves; theatrical lighting for the sight ritual; content-capture rig for KOL / press visits.

Theatrical lighting design across the four zones — premium, low-key, drawing visitors into each ritual; emphasis on the sight ritual showcasing the coffee inside the machine; daylight balance for KOL photo content.
Premium retail area for TABLÉ pre-order / purchase; display plinths for the machine and coffee, consultation seating area; expert demo counter; secure stock storage.
Trained baristas/operators serving TABLÉ coffee to visitors throughout the experience — c.50–70 visitors per cycle; consumables (cups, beans, water filtration); waste flow.
Trained TABLÉ experts to explain features in the retail area; ritual hosts in each zone; meet-and-greet for media + KOLs + tastemakers; project manager across the launch window.

Battersea Power Station (preferred) — secure a footprint large enough for the 12m circular install + retail + queueing; landlord agreement for premium experience; alternates Coal Drops Yard or Old Truman Brewery if Battersea is not viable.
Hero stills + reels of the installation; KOL/tastemaker visit capture; tier-1 media press kit; same-day social edits to amplify earned media.

RAMS for a premium public-access installation at Battersea — public-liability, fire-loading of finishes, electrical sign-off on lighting + AV + scent diffusion, crowd flow within the 12m circle (50–70 capacity).
Truck movements for the modular structure; storage for the asset post-launch (key consideration since modular reuse is in scope); install + de-rig at Battersea with venue timing constraints.

topQuestions: What is the budget envelope? The deck is silent on it but the spec (12m architectural install, premium finish, tier-1 KOL audience) is firmly in £150k+ territory before retail and operating costs.
How long does the installation live at Battersea — single-night launch event or multi-day public-access window? Materially changes staffing, H&S, and logistics.
Is the retail area transactional (taking actual orders + payment) or is it the tracked-discount alternative described in the deck? Affects fixtures, staffing and licence requirements.
